elevator_pitch: Repo Church turns agentic repository work into a Bible-backed lifecycle with deterministic gates, evidence ledgers, specialist contracts, and ship-ready handoffs.

leverage.primary_source: repo-local lifecycle artifacts that compound across phases
leverage.what_compounds_with_use: requirements, ledgers, evidence, handoffs, validation reports, and refresh records become reusable context
leverage.what_gets_harder_to_copy: the interaction between portable skills, deterministic state, quality gates, and accumulated project doctrine

market.buyer: agent-heavy repo owners and technical operators
market.urgent_pain: agent work often drifts from requirements, skips proof, and becomes hard to resume or review
market.user: coding agents, skill authors, and repo maintainers
market.why_now: agentic coding adoption makes durable repo-local process and evidence more valuable than one-off prompts

one_sentence_moat: Its moat is the self-reinforcing combination of repo-local doctrine, deterministic lifecycle state, proof-bearing ledgers, and installable agent skill contracts that make quality gates executable instead of aspirational.

customer_evidence: User requested a self-hosted E2E meta-analysis to prove the framework can improve itself through its intended lifecycle
local_evidence: README.md documents the installable lifecycle and core loop
skills/church/scripts/church.py implements state, moat, ledger, context, lifecycle, and quality checks
tests/test_church_cli.py verifies CLI behavior and failure-mode guardrails
tests/test_church_plugin_assets.py verifies staged command and specialist profile assets
market_evidence: Local repo evidence shows the package addresses agent workflow governance; external competitor freshness is deferred to roadmap P03
missing_evidence: Current dated competitor research for public positioning
Third-party user adoption data beyond this local repository

risks: Ceremony without enforcement if lifecycle gates are not tested end to end
Overclaiming market moat without current competitor research
Context bloat if Bible artifacts are not paired with deterministic summaries

sustainability.data_or_learning_loop: each phase refreshes requirements, gaps, validation evidence, and next-phase anchors
sustainability.defensibility: trust from repeatable proof that gates reject unsupported progress
sustainability.distribution_advantage: skills.sh-compatible package with staged commands and specialist profiles
sustainability.ecosystem_lock_in: low vendor lock-in by design; lock-in comes from useful repo-local artifacts rather than runtime dependence
sustainability.switching_costs: repo history, Bible requirements, ledgers, and handoffs become embedded in the project workflow
sustainability.trust_or_compliance: explicit evidence, owner, proof, and recheck fields support auditability

third_party_summary: Repo Church is a portable skills package and CLI that helps coding agents plan, verify, ship, and refresh repository work without losing traceability or proof between sessions.
validation_tests: tests/test_church_meta_lifecycle.py proves the full lifecycle reaches refresh PASS
tests/test_church_cli.py proves weak PASS records and proofless closure are rejected
npx skills add ./ --list proves install surface exposes the intended skills

wedge.entry_point: installable repo lifecycle skill package with deterministic CLI validation
wedge.first_10_users: builders who repeatedly use agents for multi-phase repository work
wedge.switching_trigger: a plan, PR, or resumed session needs proof that requirements, evidence, UAT, and ship gates were satisfied
wedge.time_to_value: first init plus context load produces state, moat, Bible scaffold, and next workflow in one session
